chunk_summary: Clinical vignettes address adolescent nutrition issues including thinness, anemia, obesity, and micronutrient deficiencies, with practical management strategies based on guidelines and diagnostic tools.
learning_objectives: Identify and manage thinness in adolescents using BMI-for-age and dietary interventions.
Diagnose and treat anemia in adolescent girls, including therapeutic iron supplementation and menstrual health assessment.
Stage and manage obesity in adolescents with lifestyle modifications and metabolic screening.
Recognize and treat vitamin B12 deficiency in strict vegetarians with neurologic symptoms.
key_facts: BMI-for-age below -2 SD indicates thinness; assess height velocity and pubertal status.
Therapeutic iron for anemia: two IFA tablets daily for 3 months, with follow-up hemoglobin checks in 2–4 weeks.
Obesity management includes zero sugar-sweetened beverages, 60 minutes/day activity, and 8–10 hours sleep.
Vitamin B12 deficiency with neurologic symptoms requires immediate replacement therapy, often IM for severe cases.
Avoid folate-only treatment in macrocytosis to prevent masking B12 deficiency.
Screening tests for obesity include fasting lipids, glycemic markers, and ALT.
reference_quote: Therapeutic iron supplementation for anemia includes two daily IFA tablets for 3 months, with follow-up hemoglobin checks in 2–4 weeks. (Source: Dietary Guidelines for Indians, ICMR-NIN)
document_key_topics: Vitamin B12 deficiency diagnosis and treatment
